name: Spicy Thai Meatballs with Crispy Noodles
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Chopped fresh cilantro or parsley
1 small Carrot, peeled and thinly sliced or cut into thin sticks
recipeInstructions:
Source: MAINBEEF.ZIP
Vegetable oil Fresh cilantro or parsley sprigs, optional
1. Pour vegetable oil into a 3-quart saucepan to a depth of 1 inch. Heat over medium-high heat until the oil reaches 375 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, combine ground pork, egg, finely chopped dry-roasted peanuts, fresh cilantro or parsley, and salt. Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon.
3. Shape the mixture into small balls (about 1-inch diameter).
4. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add the meatballs and cook for about 12 minutes, turning them frequently until they are well-browned on all sides.
5. Add 1 cup of water to the skillet, stirring to loosen the brown bits from the bottom. Bring to a boil.
6. Reduce the heat to low. Simmer, covered, for 5 to 10 minutes until the meatballs are cooked through.
7. In the saucepan with hot oil, add cellophane noodles in small amounts and cook each batch for about 20 seconds until they puff up and double in size. Use a slotted spoon to remove the noodles to paper towels to drain.
8. Once the meatballs are cooked, stir in peanut butter, grated lemon peel, and ground red pepper. Cook for an additional minute until heated through.
9. Arrange the fried noodles on a serving platter. Spoon the meatballs over the noodles and arrange cucumbers and carrots decoratively around the edge. Garnish with cilantro sprigs if desired. Makes 4 servings.
Note: Cellophane noodles are sometimes called bean thread noodles and can be found in the Oriental sections of supermarkets.
